image-alt.title: Image missing description
image-alt.description: This image has no alt text. Screen readers can't tell blind visitors what this image shows.

fix.generic: Add a description to this image in your website editor. Describe what the image shows in 1-2 sentences.
fix.wordpress: In WordPress: click the image → fill in the 'Alt Text' field in the right sidebar.
fix.squarespace: In Squarespace: click the image → click the pencil icon → fill in 'Image Alt Text'.
fix.google_sites: In Google Sites: click the image → click the three dots → 'Alt text' → enter description.
fix.civicplus: In CivicPlus: edit the page → click the image → fill in the 'Alternate Text' field.
fix.finalsite: In Finalsite Composer: click the image → 'Image Properties' → fill in 'Alt Text'.

color-contrast.title: Text hard to read (low contrast)
color-contrast.description: The text color is too similar to the background color, making it hard to read for people with low vision.

fix.generic: Change the text or background color so there is more contrast between them. Use a contrast checker tool to verify.
fix.wordpress: In WordPress: edit the block → change text or background color in the block settings sidebar.
fix.squarespace: In Squarespace: edit the section → adjust colors in the Design panel.
fix.google_sites: In Google Sites: select the text → change the text color from the formatting toolbar.
fix.civicplus: In CivicPlus: edit the page → select the text → adjust colors in the editor toolbar.
fix.finalsite: In Finalsite: edit the element → change colors in the style settings.

link-name.title: Link has no descriptive text
link-name.description: This link has no text that describes where it goes. Screen reader users won't know what this link does.

fix.generic: Add descriptive text to the link. Avoid 'click here' or 'read more' — describe the destination.

html-has-lang.title: Page missing language
html-has-lang.description: The page doesn't specify what language it's written in. Screen readers need this to pronounce words correctly.

fix.generic: Add lang="en" to the <html> tag in your page template.
fix.wordpress: This is usually set by your theme. Check Settings → General → Site Language.
fix.squarespace: Squarespace sets this automatically. If missing, contact Squarespace support.

label.title: Form field missing label
label.description: This form field (text box, dropdown, etc.) has no label. Screen reader users won't know what to enter.

fix.generic: Add a visible label to every form field. The label should describe what information to enter.

button-name.title: Button has no label
button-name.description: This button has no text or label. Screen reader users won't know what this button does.

fix.generic: Add text inside the button, or add an aria-label attribute describing the button's action.

document-title.title: Page has no title
document-title.description: The page is missing a title. Screen readers announce the page title when users navigate to a new page.

fix.generic: Add a <title> tag in the page's <head> section. It should describe the page content.
fix.wordpress: In WordPress: edit the page → set the title at the top. WordPress generates the <title> tag.
fix.squarespace: In Squarespace: edit the page → Settings → SEO → set the SEO Title.

heading-order.title: Heading levels skip
heading-order.description: Headings skip a level (e.g., H1 to H3). This makes it harder for screen reader users to understand the page structure.

fix.generic: Use headings in order: H1 → H2 → H3. Don't skip levels for visual styling — use CSS instead.

list.title: List not properly structured
list.description: This list isn't using proper HTML list tags. Screen readers may not announce it as a list.

fix.generic: Use <ul> or <ol> tags for lists instead of formatting text with dashes or numbers.

region.title: Content not in a landmark
region.description: Some page content isn't inside a landmark region (header, nav, main, footer). This makes it harder for screen reader users to navigate.

fix.generic: Wrap page content in semantic HTML elements: <header>, <nav>, <main>, <footer>.
